name: empy

metadata.name: empy
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
EmPy is a powerful, robust and mature
templating system for inserting Python code in template text.  EmPy
takes a source document, processes it, and produces output.  This is
accomplished via expansions, which are signals to the EmPy system
where to act and are indicated with markup.  Markup is set off by a
customizable prefix (by default the at sign, `@`).  EmPy can expand
arbitrary Python expressions, statements and control structures in
this way, as well as a variety of additional special forms.  The
remaining textual data is sent to the output, allowing Python to be
used in effect as a markup language.

documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

documentation: Help subsystem for EmPy.
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: emhelp.prepare
documentation:
Lazily initialize the usage data and tables only if they're
actually needed.  This is a standalone function so there's less
misleading indentation.  Idempotent.
functions

functions.name: emhelp.main
documentation: main

classes.name: emhelp.Entry

methods.name: emhelp.Entry.__init__
documentation:
Create an entry for a help topic.  Arguments are:

- raw: The left side raw value: list[str] | str
- right: The right side base value: str
- var: The configuration variable, if any: Optional[str]
- val: The boolean value of the variable, if any: Optional[bool]
- env: The environment variable name, if any: Optional[str]
- arg: The command line option arguments, if any: Optional[str]
- ord: The Unicode code point value(s), if any: Optional[int]
- ex: A practical example, if any: Optional[str]
- fun: An optional function to format the resulting string:
  Optional[Callable]
- sim: Whether or not the option entry is simple (-x): bool

methods.name: emhelp.Entry.format
documentation: Format the raw list.  Override in subclasses.

An entry in one of the usage tables.  This exists to allow
optional annotations or processing for each entry.
Entry ...

classes.name: emlib.IdentityFilter
documentation: A filter which just sends any output straight through to its sink.

classes.name: emlib.FunctionFilter
documentation:
A filter that works simply by pumping its input through a
function which maps strings into strings.

classes.name: emlib.IndentFilter
documentation:
Automatically indent a fixed number of spaces after every
newline.

classes.name: emlib.StringFilter
documentation:
A filter that takes a lookup table (e.g., string, list, or
dictionary) and filters any incoming data through it via
`str.translate`.

classes.name: emlib.BufferedFilter
documentation:
A buffered filter is one that doesn't modify the source data
sent to the sink, but instead holds it for a time.  The standard
variety only sends the data along when it receives a flush
command.

classes.name: emlib.SizeBufferedFilter
documentation:
A size-buffered filter only in fixed size chunks (excepting the
final chunk).

classes.name: emlib.FullyBufferedFilter
documentation:
A maximally-buffered filter only lets its data through on the final
close.  It ignores flushes.

classes.name: emlib.DelimitedFilter
documentation:
A delimited filter only lets data through when it sees
whole lines.

classes.name: emlib.LineDelimitedFilter
documentation:
A line-delimited filter only lets data through when it sees
whole lines.

classes.name: emlib.DroppingFilter
documentation:
A filter which drops any chunks that match the provided list of
chunks to ignore.

classes.name: emlib.SplittingFilter
documentation: A filter that splits the output stream n ways.

methods.name: emlib.Details.accumulate
documentation:
Accumulate the results of a method or attribute in a
dictionary.  If the argument is None, just return the
attribute; if not, treat it as a function and call it
with the args and kwargs.  If attr is not None, access
that named attribute on the result before returning.  If
func is not None, pass the result through the function
before storing.

documentation: In Python 2.x, exec is a statement; in Python 3.x, it's a function.  Make

functions.name: em._installPathFinder
documentation:
Install the path finder into the importlib machinery to support
EmPy modules.  Use the specified meta path index (nonnegative for an
insertion point, negative to append).  Idempotent.

documentation: Write some details, using the details subsystem if available.
functions

functions.name: em.expand
documentation:
Do a self-contained expansion of the given source data,
creating and shutting down an interpreter dedicated to the task.
Expects the same keyword arguments as the Interpreter constructor.
Additionally, 'name' will identify the expansion filename and
'locals', if present, represents the locals dictionary to use.
The sys.stdout object is saved off and then replaced before this
function returns.  Any exception that occurs will be raised to the
caller.

functions.name: em.invoke
documentation:
Run a standalone instance of an EmPy interpreter with the given
command line arguments.  See the Interpreter constructor for the
keyword arguments.

documentation: main

classes.name: em.Root
documentation:
The root class of all EmPy class hierarchies.  It defines a default
__repr__ method which will work appropriately whether or not the subclass
defines a __str__ method.  Very old versions of Python 2.x won't
print the proper __str__ form, but so be it.
Root

methods.name: em.Configuration.initialize
documentation:
Setup the declarations and definitions for the defined
attributes.
Initialization.

documentation: Do a sanity check on the configuration settings.

methods.name: em.Configuration.declare
documentation: Declare the configuration attribute.
Access.

methods.name: em.Configuration.define
documentation:
Define a configuration attribute with the given name, type
specification, initial value, and description.  If none is true, None
is a legal value.  Also, allow an optional corresponding environment
variable, and, if present, an optional blank variable to set the
value to if the environment variable is defined but is blank.  If
both env and func are present, call the function to set the
environment variable.  Finally, if invert is true, invert the
(bool) environment variable.  Additionally, if the type specification
is or contains toString, convert the value to a proper string.

methods.name: em.Configuration.has
documentation: Is this name a valid configuration attribute?

documentation: Get this (valid, existing) configuration attribute.

methods.name: em.Configuration.update
documentation: Update the configuration by keyword arguments.

methods.name: em.Configuration.clone
documentation:
Create a distinct copy of this configuration.  Make it deep if

methods.name: em.Configuration.run
documentation: Run some configuration variable assignment statements.
Configuration file loading.

methods.name: em.Configuration.load
documentation:
Update the configuration by loading from a resource
file.  If required is true, raise an exception; if false,
ignore; if None, use the default in this configuration.
Return whether or not the load succeeded.

methods.name: em.Configuration.path
documentation:
Attempt to load several resource paths, either as a
list of paths or a delimited string of paths.  If required
is true, raise an exception; if false, ignore; if None,
use the default in this configuration.

methods.name: em.Configuration.hasEnvironment
documentation:
Is the current environment variable defined in the environment?
The value does not matter.
Environment.

methods.name: em.Configuration.environment
documentation:
Get the value of the given environment variable, or default

methods.name: em.Configuration.escaped

Adjust the factory to take into account a non-default

methods.name: em.Configuration.getFactory
documentation:
Get a factory (creating one if one has not yet been

methods.name: em.Configuration.resetFactory

methods.name: em.Configuration.hasBinary
documentation: Is binary/Unicode file open support enabled?

methods.name: em.Configuration.enableBinary
documentation:
Enable binary/Unicode file open support.  This is needed in
Python 2.x for Unicode support.  If major/minor is present, only
enable it implicitly if this is in fact Python 2.x.

methods.name: em.Configuration.disableBinary
documentation: Disable binary/Unicode support for this configuration.

methods.name: em.Configuration.isDefaultEncodingErrors
documentation:
Are both of the encoding/errors combinations the default?
If either passed value is None the value in this configuration
is what is checked.  Check for input if asInput is true;
otherwise check output.
File I/O.

methods.name: em.Configuration.getDefaultEncoding
documentation: What is the default encoding?

methods.name: em.Configuration.open
em.Configuration.reconfigure
documentation:
Reconfigure an existing file (e.g., sys.stdout)) with the same

methods.name: em.Configuration.install
documentation:
Install EmPy module support, if possible.  Mark a flag the first

methods.name: em.Configuration.significatorReString
documentation:
Return a string that can be compiled into a regular

methods.name: em.Configuration.significatorRe

Return a regular expression object with the given

methods.name: em.Configuration.significatorFor

methods.name: em.Configuration.setContextFormat

Set the context format, auto-detecting which

methods.name: em.Configuration.renderContext

methods.name: em.Configuration.calculateIconsSignature
documentation:
Calculate a signature of the icons dict.  If the value
changes, it's likely (but not certain) that the underlying
dict has changed.  The signature will always differ from
None.
Icons.

methods.name: em.Configuration.signIcons
documentation: Sign the icons dict.

methods.name: em.Configuration.transmogrifyIcons
documentation:
Process the icons and make sure any keys' prefixes are
backfilled with Nones.  Call this method after modifying
icons.

methods.name: em.Configuration.validateIcons
documentation:
If the icons have not been transmogrified yet, do so
and store their signature for future reference.

methods.name: em.Configuration.initializeEmojiModules
documentation:
Initialize the emoji modules.  If moduleNames is not
specified, check the defaults.  Idempotent.
Emojis.

methods.name: em.Configuration.substituteEmoji
documentation:
Substitute emojis from the provided string.  Return
the resulting substitution or None.

methods.name: em.Configuration.isSuccessCode
documentation: Does this exit code indicate success?
Exit codes and errors.

methods.name: em.Configuration.isExitError
documentation: Is this error a SystemExit?

methods.name: em.Configuration.errorToExitCode
documentation:
Determine the exit code (can be a string) from the
error.  If the error is None, then it is success.

methods.name: em.Configuration.isNotAnError
documentation:
Is this error None (no error) or does it indicate a
successful exit?

methods.name: em.Configuration.formatError
documentation: Format an error into a string for printing.

methods.name: em.Configuration.printTraceback
documentation: Debugging.

class_variables.name: em.Configuration.unwantedGlobalsKeys
documentation: None = pseudomodule name

class_variables.name: em.Configuration.tokens
documentation: list of token factories; intialized below

class_variables.name: em.Configuration._initialized
documentation: change only in instances

The configuration encapsulates all the defaults and parameterized
behavior of an interpreter.  When created, an interpreter is assigned
a configuration; multiple configurations can be shared between different
interpreters.  To override the defaults of an interpreter, create a
Configuration instance and then modify its attributes.
Configuration

classes.name: em.File

File ...

A simple class that supports all the file-like object methods

A simple class which wraps around a delegate file-like object

classes.name: em.UncloseableFile
documentation:
A delegating file class that lets through everything except

classes.name: em.ProxyFile

methods.name: em.ProxyFile.__init__
documentation:
Create a new proxy file object with bottom as the
underlying stream.  If wrapper is not None (and is an instance
of (a subclass of) a DelegatingFile), we should wrap and
unwrap the bottom file with it for protection.

methods.name: em.ProxyFile.write
documentation: File methods.

methods.name: em.ProxyFile.close
documentation:
Close the current file.  If the current file is the bottom,
then flush it (don't close it) and dispose of it.

methods.name: em.ProxyFile._EmPy_enabled
documentation: Is the top interpreter enabled?
Stack management.

methods.name: em.ProxyFile._EmPy_current
documentation: Get the current interpreter, or None.

methods.name: em.ProxyFile._EmPy_top
documentation: Get the current stream (not interpreter) to write to.

methods.name: em.ProxyFile._EmPy_shouldWrap
documentation: Should the bottom file be wrapped and unwrapped?
Bottom file protection.

methods.name: em.ProxyFile._EmPy_wrap
documentation: Wrap the bottom file in a delegate.

methods.name: em.ProxyFile._EmPy_unwrap
documentation:
Unwrap the bottom file from the delegate, and unlink the
delegate.

methods.name: em.ProxyFile._EmPy_evocare
documentation:
Do the EmPy magic:  Increment or decrement the reference
count.  Either way, return the current reference count.
Beware of Exodus!
Special.

The proxy file object that is intended to take the place of
sys.stdout.  The proxy can manage a stack of interpreters (and
their file object streams) it is writing to, and an underlying
bottom file object.

methods.name: em.Diversion.preferFile
documentation:
Would this particular diversion prefer to be treated as a
file (true) or a string (false)?  This allows future
optimization of diversions into actual files if they get
overly large.
These methods are specific to diversions.

The representation of an active diversion.  Diversions act as

Diversion

methods.name: em.Stream.create
documentation:
Create a diversion if one does not already exist, but do not
divert to it yet.  Return the diversion.

methods.name: em.Stream.retrieve
documentation:
Retrieve the given diversion.  If an additional argument is
provided, return that instead of raising on a nonexistent
diversion.

methods.name: em.Stream.divert
documentation: Start diverting.

methods.name: em.Stream.undivert
documentation: Undivert a particular diversion.

methods.name: em.Stream.drop
documentation: Drop the specified diversion.

methods.name: em.Stream.undivertAll
documentation: Undivert all pending diversions.

methods.name: em.Stream.dropAll
documentation: Eliminate all existing diversions.

A wrapper around an (output) file object which supports
diversions and filtering.
Stream

classes.name: em.Context

methods.name: em.Context.reset
documentation: Reset the context to the start.

methods.name: em.Context.track
documentation:
Track the information for the substring in [start, end) in
the context and mark it pending.

methods.name: em.Context.accumulate
documentation:
Accumulate the pending information and incorporate it into
the total.

methods.name: em.Context.save
documentation: Take a snapshot of the current context.

methods.name: em.Context.restore
documentation: Restore from another context.

methods.name: em.Context.render
documentation:
Render the context with the given format.  If
useFormatMethod is true, use the format method; if false, use
the % operator.  If useFormatMethod is None, try to trivially
auto-detect given the format.

A simple interpreter context, storing only the current data.
It is not intended to be modified.
Context

classes.name: em.Token
documentation: An element resulting from parsing.
Token ...

classes.name: em.TextToken
documentation: A chunk of text not containing markups.

classes.name: em.ExpansionToken

class_variables.name: em.ExpansionToken.last
documentation: subclasses should always define a first class attribute
A token that involves an expansion.

classes.name: em.CommentToken
documentation: The abstract base class for the comment tokens.

classes.name: em.LineCommentToken
documentation: A line comment markup: ``@# ... NL``

classes.name: em.InlineCommentToken
documentation: An inline comment markup: ``@* ... *``

classes.name: em.LiteralToken
documentation:
The abstract base class of the literal tokens.  If used as a
concrete token class, it will expand to the first character.

classes.name: em.WhitespaceToken
documentation: A whitespace markup: ``@ WS``

classes.name: em.SwitchToken
documentation: Base class for the enable/disable tokens.

classes.name: em.EnableToken
documentation: An enable output markup: ``@+ ... NL``

classes.name: em.DisableToken
documentation: An disable output markup: ``@- ... NL``

classes.name: em.PrefixToken

class_variables.name: em.PrefixToken.first
documentation: prefix
A prefix markup: ``@@``

classes.name: em.BackquoteToken
documentation: A backquote markup: ``@`...```

classes.name: em.ExecutionToken
documentation:
The abstract base class for execution tokens (expressions,
statements, controls, etc.)

classes.name: em.ExpressionToken
documentation: An expression markup: ``@(...)``

classes.name: em.SimpleExpressionToken
documentation:
A simple expression markup: `@x`, `@x.y`, `@x(y)`, `@x[y]`,
`@f{...}`

classes.name: em.InPlaceToken
documentation: An in-place markup: ``@$...$...$``

classes.name: em.StatementToken
documentation: A statement markup: ``@{...}``

classes.name: em.ControlToken

methods.name: em.ControlToken.subscan
documentation: Do a subscan for contained tokens.

methods.name: em.ControlToken.build
documentation:
Process the list of subtokens and divide it up into a list
of chains, returning that list.  Allowed specifies a list of
the only secondary markup types which are allowed.

methods.name: em.ControlToken.subrun
documentation: Execute a list of tokens.

methods.name: em.ControlToken.run_if
documentation: Type handlers.

inner_classes.name: em.ControlToken.Chain
documentation:
A chain of tokens with a starting token and the rest
that follow.
A control markup: ``@[...]``

classes.name: em.CodedToken

methods.name: em.CodedToken.recode
documentation:
Convert a lookup table entry into a string.  A value can be a string
itself, an integer corresponding to a code point, or a 2-tuple, the
first value of which is one of the above (the second is a
description).
The abstract base class for a token that supports codings.

classes.name: em.EscapeToken

methods.name: em.EscapeToken.string
documentation:
Return a general hexadecimal escape sequence rather than
the exact one that was input.
An escape markup: ``@\...``

classes.name: em.DiacriticToken
documentation: A diacritic markup: ``@^...``

classes.name: em.IconToken
documentation: An icon markup: ``@|...``

classes.name: em.EmojiToken
documentation: An emoji markup: ``@:...:``

classes.name: em.ContextToken
documentation: A base class for the context tokens.

classes.name: em.ContextNameToken
documentation: A context name change markup: ``@?...``

classes.name: em.ContextLineToken
documentation: A context line change markup: ``@!...``

classes.name: em.ExtensionToken
documentation:
An extension markup, used for all customizable markup:
`@((...))`, `@[[...]]`, `@{{...}}`, `@<...>`, etc.

classes.name: em.Factory

methods.name: em.Factory.addToken
documentation: Add another token class to the factory.

methods.name: em.Factory.removeToken
documentation:
Remove token(s) from the mapping by first, which can be a string
first or a list of strings firsts.

methods.name: em.Factory.adjust
documentation:
Adjust this factory to swap the markup for a non-default
prefix, if necessary.

methods.name: em.Factory.addendum
documentation:
An optional addendum about unsupported markup sequence (for
compatibility or future notes).

Turn a first character sequence into a token class.  Token
classes have a first attribute which is either None to indicate
whatever the current prefix is; a string in angle brackets to
indicate a special test; or a character sequence; or a list of
character sequences.  Token classes are then retrieved by lookup
table, or special test.  Initialize this meta-factory with a list of
factory classes and it will automatically setup the lookup tables
based on their first attributes.
Factory

classes.name: em.Scanner

methods.name: em.Scanner.advance
documentation: Advance the pointer count characters.
Meta.

methods.name: em.Scanner.set
documentation:
Start the scanner digesting a new batch of data; start the pointer
over from scratch.

methods.name: em.Scanner.feed
documentation: Feed some more data to the scanner.

methods.name: em.Scanner.acquire
documentation: Lock the scanner so it doesn't destroy data on sync.

methods.name: em.Scanner.release
documentation: Unlock the scanner.

methods.name: em.Scanner.track
documentation: Accumulate the moved pointer into the context.

methods.name: em.Scanner.accumulate
documentation: Update the accumulated context into the actual context.

methods.name: em.Scanner.rectify
documentation: Reset the read head and trim down the buffer.

methods.name: em.Scanner.sync
documentation: Sync up the buffer with the read head.

methods.name: em.Scanner.unsync
documentation: Undo changes; reset the read head.

methods.name: em.Scanner.rest
documentation: Get the remainder of the buffer.

methods.name: em.Scanner.chop
documentation:
Chop the first count + slop characters off the front, and
return the first count, advancing the pointer past them.  If
count is not specified, then return everything.
Active.

methods.name: em.Scanner.enclosure
documentation:
Consume and return the next enclosure (text wrapped
in the given delimiters).  The delimiters can be repeated.

methods.name: em.Scanner.read
documentation:
Read count chars starting from start; raise a transient
error if there aren't enough characters remaining.

methods.name: em.Scanner.find
documentation: Find the next occurrence of the substring, or return -1.

methods.name: em.Scanner.trivial
documentation:
Find the first occurrence of the substring where the
previous character is _not_ the escape character `\`).

methods.name: em.Scanner.last
documentation:
Find the first character that is _not_ one of the specified
characters.

methods.name: em.Scanner.grab
documentation:
Find the next occurrence of the substring and chop the
intervening characters, disposing the substring found.

methods.name: em.Scanner.next
documentation:
Scan for the next occurrence of one of the characters in
the target string; optionally, make the scan mandatory.

methods.name: em.Scanner.check
documentation:
Scan for the next single or triple quote, optionally with
the specified archetype.  Return the found quote or None.

methods.name: em.Scanner.quote
documentation: Scan for the end of the next quote.

methods.name: em.Scanner.nested
documentation:
Scan from start for an ending sequence, respecting entries and exits
only.

methods.name: em.Scanner.complex
documentation:
Scan from start for an ending sequence, respecting quotes,
entries and exits.

methods.name: em.Scanner.word
documentation: Scan from start for a simple word.

methods.name: em.Scanner.phrase
documentation:
Scan from start for a phrase (e.g., 'word', 'f(a, b, c)',
'a[i]', or combinations like 'x[i](a)'.

methods.name: em.Scanner.simple
documentation:
Scan from start for a simple expression, which consists of
one more phrases separated by dots.  Return a tuple giving the
end of the expression and a list of tuple pairs consisting of
the simple expression extensions found, if any.

methods.name: em.Scanner.one
documentation:
Parse, scan, and return one token, or None if the scanner
is empty.  If the firebreaks argument is supplied, chop up
text tokens before a character in that string.

methods.name: em.Scanner.all
documentation: Yield a sequence of all tokens.

A scanner holds a buffer for lookahead parsing and has the
ability to scan for special symbols and indicators in that
buffer.
Scanner

classes.name: em.Command

methods.name: em.Command.process
documentation: Run the command.
A generic high-level processing command.
Command ...

classes.name: em.ImportCommand
documentation: Import a Python module.

classes.name: em.DefineCommand
documentation: Define a Python variable.

classes.name: em.StringCommand
documentation: Define a Python string variable.

classes.name: em.DocumentCommand
documentation: Read and execute an EmPy document.

classes.name: em.ExecuteCommand
documentation: Execute a Python statement.

classes.name: em.FileCommand
documentation: Load an execute a Python file.

classes.name: em.ExpandCommand
documentation: Execute a Python statement.

classes.name: em.Plugin

methods.name: em.Plugin.attach
documentation:
Attach this plugin to an interpreter.  This needs to be a
separate step since some methods require access to the
interpreter.

methods.name: em.Plugin.detach
documentation:
Detach this plugin from any interpreter.  This breaks any
cyclical links between the interpreter and core.

A plugin is an object owned by an interpreter that has a back-reference
to it.
Plugin

classes.name: em.Core

methods.name: em.Core.quote
documentation: Find the right quote for this code.

methods.name: em.Core.evaluate
documentation: Evaluate an expression and return it.
Implementation (override these)

methods.name: em.Core.execute
documentation: Execute a statement(s); return value ignored.

methods.name: em.Core.serialize
documentation: Return the string representation of an object.

methods.name: em.Core.define
documentation: Implement @[def ...] markup; return value ignored.

methods.name: em.Core.match
documentation: Implement @[match ...] markup; return value ignored.

A core encapsulates the functionality of the underlying language
(Python by default).  To create an object where this these are
native methods to that class, derive a class from Core but do not
call its constructor.
Core

classes.name: em.Extension
documentation:
An extension plugin that the interpreter will defer to for
non-standard markup.
Extension

classes.name: em.Interpreter

methods.name: em.Interpreter.__init__
documentation:
Accept keyword arguments only, so users will never have to
worry about the ordering of arguments.
Construction, initialization, destruction.

methods.name: em.Interpreter._initialize
documentation:
Initialize the interpreter with the given arguments (all of
which have defaults).  The number and order of arguments here
is subject to change.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.reset
documentation:
Completely reset the interpreter state.  If clearStacks is
true, wipe the call stacks.  If immediately is true, declare
the interpreter ready.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.ready
documentation: Declare the interpreter ready for normal operations.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.finalize
documentation: Execute any remaining final routines.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.install
documentation: Install any global apparatus necessary.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.succeeded
documentation:
Did the interpreter succeed?  That is, is the logged
error not an error?

methods.name: em.Interpreter.pause
documentation: Pause (at the end of processing).

methods.name: em.Interpreter.shutdown
documentation:
Declare this interpreting session over; close all the
stream file objects, and if this is the last interpreter,
uninstall the proxy.  This method is idempotent.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.check
documentation: Check the verify this interpreter is still alive.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.failed
documentation:
Has this interpreter had an error (which we should not
ignore)?

methods.name: em.Interpreter.enable
documentation: Writeable file-like methods.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.serialize
documentation:
Output the string version of an object, or a special token if
it is None.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.top
documentation: Get the top stream.
Stream stack-related activity.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.include
documentation: Do an include pass on a file or filename.
Entry-level processing.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.expand
documentation:
Do an explicit expansion on a subordinate stream in a
new context.  If dispatch is true, dispatch any exception
through the interpreter; otherwise just reraise.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.go
documentation: Execute an interpreter stack at a high level.
High-level processing.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.protect
documentation:
Wrap around an application of a callable in a new
context (named name).

methods.name: em.Interpreter.interact
documentation: Perform interaction.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.file
documentation: Parse a file according to the current buffering strategy.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.fileLines
documentation: Parse the entire contents of a file-like object, line by line.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.fileChunks
documentation:
Parse the entire contents of a file-like object, in
buffered chunks.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.fileFull
documentation:
Parse the entire contents of a file-like object, in one big
chunk.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.import_
documentation: Import an EmPy module.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.string
documentation: Parse a string.  Cleans up after itself.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.safe
documentation:
Do a protected parse.  Catch transient parse errors; if
final is true, then make a final pass with a terminator,
otherwise ignore the transient parse error (more data is
pending).  Return true if the scanner is exhausted or if an
error has occurred.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.parse
documentation:
Parse and run as much from this scanner as possible.  Return
true if the scanner ran out of tokens.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.process
documentation: Process a command.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.processAll
documentation: Process a sequence of commands.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.tokens
documentation:
Do an explicit result on a sequence of tokens.
Cleans up after itself.
Medium-level processing.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.quote
documentation:
Quote the given string so that if it were expanded it would
evaluate to the original.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.escape
documentation:
Escape a string so that nonprintable or non-ASCII characters
are replaced with compatible EmPy expansions.  Also treat
characters in more as escapes.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.tokenize
documentation:
Take an lvalue string and return a name or a (possibly recursive)
list of names.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.atomic
documentation: Do an atomic assignment.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.multi
documentation: Do a (potentially recursive) assignment.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.assign
documentation: Do a potentially complex (including tuple unpacking) assignment.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.significate
documentation: Declare a significator.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.clause
documentation:
Given the string representation of an except clause, turn
it into a 2-tuple consisting of the class name or tuple of
names, and either a variable name or None.  If the
representation is None, then it's all exceptions and no name.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.dictionary
documentation:
Given a string representing a key-value argument list, turn
it into a dictionary.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.literal
documentation: Process a string literal.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.functional
documentation:
Handle a functional expression like @f{x} and return the
result.  code is the Python code to evaluate tokensLists is a
list of list of tokens.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.run
documentation: Run a token, tracking the current context.
Low-level evaluation.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.runSeveral
documentation: Run a sequence of tokens.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.defined
documentation:
Return a Boolean indicating whether or not the name is
defined either in the locals or the globals.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.lookup
documentation: Lookup the value of a variable.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluate an expression.  If replace is true, replace
newlines in the expression with spaces if that config
variable is set; otherwise, don't do it regardless.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.execute
documentation: Execute a statement(s).

methods.name: em.Interpreter.single
documentation:
Execute an expression or statement, just as if it were
entered into the Python interactive interpreter.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.evocare
documentation:
Try to call the EmPy special method on the proxy with the
given increment argument and return the resulting count value.
If the magic method is not present (no proxy installed) return None.
Proxy.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.installProxy
documentation:
Install a proxy if necessary around the given output,
wrapped to be uncloseable, which can be None.  Return the
wrapped object (but not the proxy).

methods.name: em.Interpreter.uninstallProxy
documentation: Uninstall a proxy if necessary.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.checkProxy
documentation:
Check whether a proxy is installed.  Returns the
current reference count (positive means one is
installed), None (for no proxy installed), or 0 if the
proxy has been abandoned.  Thus, true means a proxy is
installed, false means one isn't.  If abandonIsError
is true, raise instead of returning 0 on abandonment.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.identify
documentation:
Identify the topmost context with a tuple of the name and
counters.
Pseudomodule routines.
Identification.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getContext
documentation: Get the top context.
Contexts.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.newContext
documentation: Create and return a new context.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.pushContext
documentation: Push a new context on the stack.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.popContext
documentation: Pop the top context.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.setContext
documentation: Replace the top context.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.setContextName
documentation: Set the name of the topmost context.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.setContextLine
documentation: Set the line number of the topmost context.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.setContextColumn
documentation: Set the column number of the topmost context.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.setContextData
documentation: Set any of the name, line, or column of the topmost context.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.restoreContext
documentation: Restore from an old context.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.clearFinalizers
documentation: Clear all finalizers.
Finalizers.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.appendFinalizer
documentation: Register a function to be called at exit.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.prependFinalizer
documentation: Register a function to be called at exit.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.fixGlobals
documentation: Reset the globals, stamping in the pseudomodule.
Globals.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.unfixGlobals
documentation: Remove the pseudomodule (if present) from the globals.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getGlobals
documentation: Retrieve the globals.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.setGlobals
documentation: Set the globals to the specified dictionary.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.updateGlobals
documentation: Merge another mapping object into this interpreter's globals.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.clearGlobals
documentation: Clear out the globals with a brand new dictionary.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.pushGlobals
documentation: Push a globals dictionary onto the history stack.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.popGlobals
documentation:
Pop a globals dictinoary off the history stack and return
it.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.saveGlobals
documentation: Save a copy of the globals off onto the history stack.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.restoreGlobals
documentation: Restore the most recently saved copy of the globals.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.flattenGlobals
documentation:
Flatten the contents of the pseudo-module into the globals
namespace.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getPrefix
documentation: Get the current prefix.
Prefix.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.setPrefix
documentation: Set the prefix.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.stopDiverting
documentation: Stop any diverting.
Diversions.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.createDiversion
documentation:
Create a diversion (but do not divert to it) if it does not
already exist.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.retrieveDiversion
documentation: Retrieve the diversion object associated with the name.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.startDiversion
documentation: Start diverting to the given diversion name.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.playDiversion
documentation: Play the given diversion and then drop it.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.replayDiversion
documentation: Replay the diversion without dropping it.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.dropDiversion
documentation: Eliminate the given diversion.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.playAllDiversions
documentation: Play all existing diversions and then drop them.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.replayAllDiversions
documentation: Replay all existing diversions without dropping them.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.dropAllDiversions
documentation: Drop all existing diversions.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getCurrentDiversionName
documentation: Get the name of the current diversion.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getAllDiversionNames
documentation: Get the names of all existing diversions.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.isExistingDiversionName
documentation: Does a diversion with this name currently exist?

methods.name: em.Interpreter.resetFilter
documentation: Reset the filter stream so that it does no filtering.
Filters.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getFilter
documentation: Get the top-level filter.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getLastFilter
documentation: Get the last filter in the current chain.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getFilterCount
documentation:
Get the number of chained filters; 0 means no active
filters.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.setFilter
documentation: Set the filter.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.prependFilter
documentation: Attach a single filter to the end of the current filter chain.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.appendFilter
documentation: Attach a single filter to the end of the current filter chain.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.setFilterChain
documentation: Set the filter.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.hasCore
documentation: Does this interpreter have a core inserted?
Core-related activity.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getCore
documentation: Get this interpreter's core or None.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.insertCore
documentation: Insert and attach the execution core.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.ejectCore
documentation: Clear the execution core, breaking a potential cyclical link.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.resetCore
documentation: Reset the execution core to the default core.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.hasExtension
documentation: Does this interpreter have an extension installed?
Extension-related activity.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.installExtension
documentation: Install an extension.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.uninstallExtension
documentation:
Uninstall any extension.  This should only be done by the
interpreter itself at shutdown time.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.invokeHook
documentation:
Invoke the hook(s) associated with the hook name, should they
exist.  Stop and return on the first hook which returns a true
result.
Hooks.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.areHooksEnabled
documentation: Return whether or not hooks are presently enabled.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.enableHooks
documentation: Enable hooks.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.disableHooks
documentation: Disable hooks.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getHooks
documentation: Get the current hooks.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.addHook
documentation: Add a new hook; optionally insert it rather than appending it.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.appendHook
documentation: Append the given hook.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.prependHook
documentation: Prepend the given hook.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.removeHook
documentation: Remove a preexisting hook.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.clearHooks
documentation: Clear all hooks.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.hasCallback
documentation: Is there a custom callback registered?
Callbacks (DEPRECATED).

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getCallback
documentation:
Get the custom markup callback registered with this
interpreter, or None.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.registerCallback
documentation: Register a custom markup callback with this interpreter.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.deregisterCallback
documentation:
Remove any previously registered custom markup callback
with this interpreter.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.invokeCallback
documentation: Call the custom markup callback.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getExitCode
documentation:
Get the exit code corresponding for the current error (if
any).
Error handling.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.exit
documentation:
Exit.  If exitCode is None, use the exit code from the
current error (which may itself be None for no error).

methods.name: em.Interpreter.reraise
documentation: Reraise an exception.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.dispatch
documentation: Dispatch an exception.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.handle
documentation: Handle an actual error that occurred.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.defaultHandler
documentation: Report an error.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getHandler
documentation: Get the current handler, or None for the default.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.setHandler
documentation:
Set the current handler.  Additionally, specify whether
errors should exit (defaults to false with a custom
handler).

methods.name: em.Interpreter.invokeHandler
documentation:
Manually invoke the error handler with the given
exception info 3-tuple or three arguments.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.initializeEmojiModules
documentation:
Determine which emoji module to use.  If moduleNames is not
specified, use the defaults.
Emojis.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getEmojiModule
documentation:
Return an abstracted emoji module by name or return
None.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.getEmojiModuleNames
documentation:
Return the emoji module names in usage in their proper
order.

methods.name: em.Interpreter.substituteEmoji
documentation: Substitute an emoji text or return None.

class_variables.name: em.Interpreter._proxy
documentation: the installed proxy or None
An interpreter can process chunks of EmPy code.
Interpreter
